description: A "Carpodacus" finch first noted because of its call: pit, pit. Face with solid dark auriculars contrasting sharply with clean white supercilium and white submoustachial. Broad brown malar stripes merging into moderately thick brown chest streaking against white background, making bird very contrasty No eyering. Bill pudgier, not as long, and somewhat stouter, than that of Cassin's Finch. Bill all dark. Bird not seen "lower" than upper wing.Behaviors: Sitting in upper branches of tall bushCall: pit-pitPlumage: imm male or female
